title: Upon Rook. Epig.
content:
Rook he sells feathers, yet he still doth cry
    Fie on this pride, this female vanity.
    Thus, though the Rook does rail against the sin,
    He loves the gain that vanity brings in.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Rook. Epig."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
